docs.title: Agronomy
docs.summary: The science and practice of crop production and soil management, integrating plant science, soil science, ecology, and farming systems.

docs.title: Annual crop
docs.summary: A crop that completes its life cycle — germination to seed production — within a single growing season and is then replanted.

docs.title: Perennial crop
docs.summary: A crop that lives for more than two years, producing over multiple seasons without replanting each cycle.

docs.title: Cultivar
docs.summary: A cultivated variety of a plant species, selected and maintained for distinct, desirable, and stable characteristics.

docs.title: Soil texture
docs.summary: The relative proportion of sand, silt, and clay particles in a soil, which strongly influences water movement, aeration, and workability.

docs.title: Soil pH
docs.summary: A measure of soil acidity or alkalinity on a logarithmic scale, affecting nutrient availability and biological activity. Optimal ranges are crop-specific.

docs.title: Macronutrient
docs.summary: A nutrient required by plants in relatively large amounts — nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, calcium, magnesium, and sulfur.

docs.title: Micronutrient
docs.summary: A nutrient required by plants in small quantities, such as iron, zinc, manganese, boron, copper, molybdenum, and chlorine.

docs.title: Integrated pest management (IPM)
docs.summary: A decision-based approach that combines monitoring, prevention, biological, cultural, physical, and — where justified — chemical controls to manage pests with r

docs.title: Economic threshold
docs.summary: The pest population level at which control action is justified to prevent the pest from causing economic loss; a core concept in integrated pest management.

docs.title: Crop rotation
docs.summary: The practice of growing different crops in sequence on the same land to manage soil fertility, break pest and disease cycles, and reduce weed pressure.

docs.title: Cover crop
docs.summary: A crop grown primarily to protect and improve the soil — reducing erosion, adding organic matter, and cycling nutrients — rather than for harvest.

docs.title: Evapotranspiration
docs.summary: The combined loss of water from soil evaporation and plant transpiration; a key quantity in estimating crop water requirements.

docs.title: Host plant
docs.summary: A plant species on which a pest or pathogen can feed, reproduce, or complete part of its life cycle.

docs.title: Inoculum
docs.summary: The pathogen material — such as spores, mycelium, or bacterial cells — capable of initiating infection in a host plant.

docs.title: Ruminant
docs.summary: A mammal, such as cattle, sheep, or goats, that digests plant material by fermenting it in a specialized four-compartment stomach before further digestion.

docs.title: Forage
docs.summary: Plant material — including grasses and legumes — grazed or harvested to feed livestock.

docs.title: Yield
docs.summary: The quantity of a crop produced per unit area, commonly expressed in tonnes or kilograms per hectare. Actual yields vary widely by variety, region, season, and

docs.title: Agricultural land
docs.summary: Share of land area that is arable, under permanent crops, or under permanent pasture.

docs.title: Agricultural land area
docs.summary: Total agricultural land area in square kilometres.

docs.title: Arable land
docs.summary: Land under temporary crops, temporary meadows, market/kitchen gardens, and temporarily fallow, as a share of land area.

docs.title: Arable land per person
docs.summary: Arable land in hectares divided by total population.

docs.title: Cereal yield
docs.summary: Cereal yield (kg/ha of harvested land) for wheat, rice, maize, barley, and other cereals.

docs.title: Cereal production
docs.summary: Total cereal production in metric tons (grain crops harvested for dry grain).

docs.title: Crop production index
docs.summary: Agricultural crop production relative to the 2014–2016 base period.

docs.title: Food production index
docs.summary: Food crop production relative to the 2014–2016 base period.

docs.title: Livestock production index
docs.summary: Livestock production relative to the 2014–2016 base period.

docs.title: Agriculture value added
docs.summary: Value added by agriculture, forestry, and fishing as a share of GDP.

docs.title: Employment in agriculture
docs.summary: Share of total employment in agriculture, forestry, and fishing.

docs.title: Fertilizer consumption
docs.summary: Fertilizer nutrient consumption per hectare of arable land.

docs.title: Forest area
docs.summary: Forest area as a share of land area.

docs.title: Irrigated agricultural land
docs.summary: Agricultural land equipped for irrigation, as a share of agricultural land.

docs.title: Average precipitation
docs.summary: Long-term average precipitation in depth (mm per year).

docs.title: Rural population
docs.summary: Share of the population living in rural areas.

docs.title: Fertilizer nutrient calculator
docs.summary: Convert between fertilizer mass and nutrient amount from a percentage grade, and between oxide (P₂O₅/K₂O) and elemental (P/K) notation.

docs.title: Seed-rate calculator
docs.summary: Estimate seeds to sow from a target plant population, germination, and field establishment — and a seed rate in kg/ha if a thousand-seed weight is given.

docs.title: Plant-density calculator
docs.summary: Plants per unit area from row and in-row spacing (and per m²/acre).

docs.title: Irrigation volume calculator
docs.summary: Net irrigation volume from a target depth and area, and gross volume from an application efficiency.

docs.title: Growing degree-days calculator
docs.summary: Accumulate a single day of growing degree-days from daily temperatures and a base temperature.

docs.title: Yield converter
docs.summary: Convert crop yields between t/ha, kg/ha, lb/acre, and commodity-specific bu/acre.

docs.title: Agricultural unit converters
docs.summary: Convert common agricultural units: area, mass, volume (US/Imperial), temperature, water depth, and application rate.
